channel.title: Computer Vision | TMU Multimedia Research Laboratory

item.title: Bronze Medal at Kaggle — Bacterial Flagellar Motors Detection
item.description:
MASc student Yufei Ren and Dr. Naimul Khan won a Bronze Medal in the Kaggle BYU Locating Bacterial Flagellar Motors in 3D Tomograms competition, placing 64th out of 1,136 teams.

The task involved detecting bacterial flagellar motor locations in 3D cryo-electron tomograms — a challenging volumetric detection problem with significant implications for structural biology. Our approach was based on MHAF-YOLO, a multi-head attention feature fusion architecture adapted for 3D detection.

Congratulations Yufei!

item.title: DynaGuide: A Generalizable Dynamic Guidance Framework for Zero-Shot Guided Unsupervised Semantic Segmentation
Best Paper Runner-Up at CVPR 2021 Workshop
item.description:
Congratulations to Julian True! His paper “Motion Vector Extrapolation for Video Object Detection” won the Best Paper Runner-Up Award at the CVPR 2021 Robust Video Understanding Workshop.

The paper demonstrates that leveraging motion vectors from compressed video can decrease object detection latency by up to 25× while maintaining detection accuracy — a significant advance for real-time video understanding on resource-constrained devices.

item.title: Intelligent Scene Understanding for Mobile Augmented Reality
item.description:
Overview

This project develops machine learning algorithms for 3D object recognition using point cloud data captured from ARKit and ARCore frameworks, enabling intelligent scene understanding and collaborative augmented reality experiences on mobile devices. In partnership with AWE Company, the work targets real-world AR applications that can semantically understand their environment in real time.

Collaborators

  • AWE Company

Funding

  • NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant (CRD)

item.title: Cloud-based Multimodal Biometrics Framework for Road Safety
item.description:
Overview

In partnership with Alcohol Countermeasure Systems, this project develops a novel cloud-based multimodal biometrics framework for continuous driver authentication and real-time drowsiness detection. By combining multiple physiological and behavioral signals, the system aims to significantly enhance road safety by identifying impaired or fatigued drivers before accidents occur.

Collaborators

  • Alcohol Countermeasure Systems

Funding

  • NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant (CRD)

item.title: Augmented Reality Framework for Museum Exhibits
item.description:
Overview

In partnership with SimentIT and the Canadian Science and Technology Museum (CSTM) in Ottawa, this project developed a cloud-based mobile augmented reality platform for museum exhibits. The system enables real-time 3D object recognition and pose estimation under challenging and variable museum lighting conditions. The technology was deployed in CSTM’s “Artifact Alley”, where visitors experience a barrier-free and natural AR experience overlaid on physical artifacts.

Collaborators

  • SimentIT
  • Canadian Science and Technology Museum (CSTM), Ottawa

Funding

  • NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant (CRD) — $450K
